narrative: CLAIM A — Stable population-level geometric descriptor. The pooled profile posterior is sharply concentrated at theta~17.25 degrees and is stable under bootstrap resampling (95% CI within 3 degrees). The posterior is identifiable at the population level because large-sample aggregation provides sufficient statistical power to resolve the small-angle signal, even though individual-level identification under sparse binning is limited. The paper can defend theta~17 degrees as a stable population-level descriptor of contextual overlap in the Gender-Science IAT.
